summary: Teaches relational database design through ER modelling, normalization, and schema construction — the foundation for all structured data analytics.
keyIdeas: Entity-Relationship diagrams map real-world objects to database tables
Normalization eliminates data redundancy and prevents update anomalies
Primary and foreign keys enforce referential integrity across tables
Schema design decisions affect query performance and data quality downstream
Good database design separates concerns: entities, relationships, and attributes
practicalIntuition: Every analytics project depends on well-structured data — bad schema design creates problems that no amount of ML can fix.
commonConfusion: Designing tables based on how you want to query them rather than how the data naturally relates — normalize first, optimize queries second.
businessMeaning: Database design is the invisible infrastructure behind every dashboard, report, and ML pipeline in an organization.

summary: Covers SQL query construction from basic selects through complex joins, aggregations, and KPI computation for business decision-making.
keyIdeas: SELECT-FROM-WHERE is the foundation of all SQL queries
JOINs combine data across tables using key relationships
GROUP BY with aggregate functions (COUNT, SUM, AVG) compute business KPIs
Subqueries and CTEs enable multi-step analytical queries
Query results must be interpreted in business context, not just presented as tables
practicalIntuition: SQL is the most universally demanded technical skill in analytics — every data role requires it regardless of industry or seniority.
commonConfusion: Writing overly complex queries when simpler joins would suffice — start with the simplest query that answers the business question.
businessMeaning: Business intelligence, reporting, and operational analytics all run on SQL — this is the skill you'll use most frequently in industry.

summary: End-to-end database analytics project: design a relational database for cruise line reservations, build it in SQL, query for business insights, and present managerial recommendations.
keyIdeas: Real database projects start with understanding the business domain before writing any SQL
ER diagram design requires mapping business entities (ships, passengers, reservations) to tables
KPI queries answer specific business questions like occupancy rates and revenue per voyage
Scenario extensions test how the database handles new business requirements
Managerial insights translate query results into actionable business recommendations
practicalIntuition: This project mirrors real analytics work: understand the business, design the data structure, query for insights, and communicate recommendations.
commonConfusion: Focusing only on getting queries to run without interpreting what the results mean for business decisions.
assignmentAnchor: Group Projects 1 & 2 (Cruise Line Database, with Hai Wang)
businessMeaning: Database analytics projects like this are the bread and butter of business intelligence and data analytics roles.
